docs.title: OSSREP - Open Source Retail Electric Provider
docs.text: Welcome to the documentation for OSSREP, an open-source platform for managing a Retail Electric Provider (REP) in the state of Texas.

docs.title: Project Overview
docs.text: OSSREP aims to provide a complete, production-ready platform for operating a retail electric provider in the ERCOT (Electric Reliability Council of Texas) market. The platform is built using modern, cloud-native technologies and follows microservices architecture principles.

docs.title: Technology Stack
docs.text: Layer Technology Backend Quarkus (Java) Frontend Angular Database PostgreSQL Documentation MkDocs Material

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Customer Management - Enrollment, account management, service addresses
  • Billing & Invoicing - Usage calculation, invoice generation, payment processing
  • Usage/Metering - Integration with Smart Meter Texas
  • ERCOT Integration - Market transactions, enrollment, settlements
  • Customer Portal - Self-service web application
  • Operations Portal - Internal administration and customer service

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Check out our Getting Started Guide to begin working with OSSREP.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: OSSREP is fully open source. We welcome contributions from the community. See our contribution guidelines for more information.

docs.title: License
docs.text: This project is open source. License details to be determined.

docs.title: Microservices
docs.text: Detailed documentation for each microservice in the OSSREP platform.

docs.title: Customer Service
docs.text:
Repository: customer-service

Manages customer accounts, contacts, and service locations.

docs.title: Responsibilities
docs.text:
• Customer account lifecycle (create, update, close)
  • Contact information management
  • Service address/premise management
  • Customer preferences and communications

docs.title: Key Entities
docs.text:
• Customer - Core customer account
  • Contact - Customer contact information
  • ServiceLocation - Physical service address (ESI ID in Texas)
  • CustomerPreference - Communication and billing preferences

docs.title: Billing Service
docs.text:
Repository: billing-service

Handles all billing, invoicing, and payment operations.

docs.title: Responsibilities
docs.text:
• Invoice generation and calculation
  • Payment processing and allocation
  • Account balance management
  • Collections and dunning workflows

docs.title: Key Entities
docs.text:
• Invoice - Customer bill
  • InvoiceLine - Line items on invoice
  • Payment - Payment transaction
  • AccountBalance - Current account state

docs.title: Usage Service
docs.text:
Repository: usage-service

Processes and manages electricity usage data.

docs.title: Responsibilities
docs.text:
• Receive and store meter readings
  • Process interval data from Smart Meter Texas
  • Calculate usage summaries
  • Provide usage data for billing

docs.title: Key Entities
docs.text:
• MeterReading - Point-in-time meter read
  • IntervalData - 15-minute interval usage
  • UsageSummary - Aggregated usage for billing periods

docs.title: Enrollment Service
docs.text:
Repository: enrollment-service

Manages customer enrollment and ERCOT market transactions.

docs.title: Responsibilities
docs.text:
• New customer enrollment (switch requests)
  • Move-in and move-out processing
  • ERCOT transaction submission and tracking
  • Enrollment status management

docs.title: Key Entities
docs.text:
• EnrollmentRequest - Customer enrollment/switch request
  • ErcotTransaction - Transaction submitted to ERCOT
  • ServiceOrder - Internal service order

docs.title: Product Service
docs.text:
Repository: product-service

Manages electricity products, rate plans, and pricing.

docs.title: Responsibilities
docs.text:
• Product catalog management
  • Rate plan configuration
  • Pricing calculation rules
  • Contract terms and conditions

docs.title: Key Entities
docs.text:
• Product - Electricity product offering
  • RatePlan - Pricing structure
  • PricingTier - Tiered pricing levels
  • Contract - Customer contract terms

docs.title: Gateway Service
docs.text:
Repository: gateway-service

API gateway for routing and cross-cutting concerns.

docs.title: Responsibilities
docs.text:
• Request routing to appropriate services
  • Authentication and authorization
  • Rate limiting
  • Request/response logging

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text: OSSREP follows a microservices architecture pattern, with each service responsible for a specific business domain within the retail electric provider operations.

docs.title: High-Level Architecture
docs.text:
graph TB
    subgraph "Frontend Applications"
        CP[Customer Portal]
        AP[Admin Portal]
    end

    subgraph "API Gateway"
        GW[Gateway Service]
    end

    subgraph "Core Services"
        CS[Customer Service]
        BS[Billing Service]
        US[Usage Service]
        ES[Enrollment Service]
        PS[Product Service]
    end

    subgraph "Integration Services"
        ERCOT[ERCOT Integration]
        SMT[Smart Meter Texas]
        PAY[Payment Gateway]
    end

    subgraph "Infrastructure"
        DB[(PostgreSQL)]
        MQ[Message Queue]
    end

    CP --> GW
    AP --> GW
    GW --> CS
    GW --> BS
    GW --> US
    GW --> ES
    GW --> PS

    CS --> DB
    BS --> DB
    US --> DB
    ES --> DB
    PS --> DB

    ES --> ERCOT
    US --> SMT
    BS --> PAY

    CS <--> MQ
    BS <--> MQ
    US <--> MQ
    ES <--> MQ

docs.title: Domain-Driven Design
docs.text:
Each microservice represents a bounded context aligned with a specific business domain:

  • Customer Context - Customer accounts, contacts, service locations
  • Billing Context - Invoices, payments, collections
  • Usage Context - Meter data, consumption tracking
  • Enrollment Context - Service requests, switches, move-ins/outs
  • Product Context - Rate plans, pricing, contracts

docs.title: Event-Driven Architecture
docs.text:
Services communicate asynchronously through events for loose coupling:

  • CustomerCreated, CustomerUpdated
  • UsageReceived, UsageProcessed
  • InvoiceGenerated, PaymentReceived
  • EnrollmentSubmitted, EnrollmentConfirmed

docs.title: API-First Design
docs.text: All services expose RESTful APIs following OpenAPI 3.0 specification.

docs.title: Repository Structure
docs.text: Repository Description ossrep.github.io Documentation site customer-service Customer management microservice billing-service Billing and invoicing microservice usage-service Usage data processing microservice enrollment-service Enrollment and ERCOT transactions product-service Products and rate plans gateway-service API gateway customer-portal Angular customer-facing application admin-portal Angular internal operations application ossrep-commons Shared libraries and utilities

docs.title: Backend - Quarkus
docs.text: Quarkus is a Kubernetes-native Java framework tailored for GraalVM and HotSpot.

docs.title: Why Quarkus?
docs.text:
• Fast startup - Critical for serverless and containerized deployments
  • Low memory footprint - Efficient resource utilization
  • Developer experience - Live reload, unified configuration
  • Cloud-native - Built for Kubernetes, excellent container support
  • Reactive support - Both imperative and reactive programming models
  • Extensions ecosystem - Rich library of integrations

docs.title: Key Extensions Used
docs.text: Extension Purpose quarkus-resteasy-reactive RESTful API endpoints quarkus-hibernate-orm-panache Database ORM with active record pattern quarkus-jdbc-postgresql PostgreSQL connectivity quarkus-smallrye-openapi OpenAPI documentation quarkus-smallrye-jwt JWT authentication quarkus-smallrye-reactive-messaging Event-driven messaging quarkus-container-image-docker Container image building

docs.title: Frontend - Angular
docs.text: Angular is a platform for building web applications.

docs.title: Why Angular?
docs.text:
• Enterprise-ready - Mature, well-supported framework
  • TypeScript - Strong typing for large codebases
  • Comprehensive - Routing, forms, HTTP, testing built-in
  • Tooling - Excellent CLI and IDE support
  • Component architecture - Reusable, testable components

docs.title: Key Libraries
docs.text: Library Purpose @angular/material UI component library @ngrx/store State management @angular/router Application routing

docs.title: Database - PostgreSQL
docs.text: PostgreSQL is a powerful open-source relational database.

docs.title: Why PostgreSQL?
docs.text:
• Reliability - ACID compliant, battle-tested
  • Features - JSON support, full-text search, extensions
  • Performance - Excellent query optimizer
  • Open source - No licensing costs
  • Ecosystem - Wide tooling and hosting support

docs.title: Database Strategy
docs.text:
• Each microservice owns its database schema
  • No direct cross-service database access
  • Flyway for schema migrations

docs.title: Containerization
docs.text:
• Docker - Container runtime
  • Kubernetes - Container orchestration (production)
  • Docker Compose - Local development

docs.title: CI/CD
docs.text:
• GitHub Actions - Automated builds, tests, deployments
  • GitHub Container Registry - Container image storage

docs.title: Messaging
docs.text: • Apache Kafka or RabbitMQ - Event streaming/messaging between services

docs.title: Observability
docs.text:
• OpenTelemetry - Distributed tracing
  • Prometheus - Metrics collection
  • Grafana - Dashboards and visualization

docs.title: Development Conversations
docs.text:
This section contains transcripts and summaries of conversations with Claude AI during the development of OSSREP. These conversations serve as:

  • Design decisions documentation - Understanding why certain architectural choices were made
  • Knowledge base - Solutions to problems encountered during development
  • Learning resource - For new contributors to understand the project evolution

docs.title: Conversations
docs.text: Date Topic Summary 2026-01-09 Project Kickoff Initial project setup, technology decisions, and architecture planning

docs.title: Adding New Conversations
docs.text:
When documenting a new conversation:

  1. Create a new file with the naming convention: YYYY-MM-DD-topic-slug.md
  2. Include the date, participants, and a brief summary at the top
  3. Organize the content by topics discussed
  4. Highlight key decisions and action items
  5. Update this index page with a link to the new conversation

docs.title: Project Kickoff
docs.text: Date: 2026-01-09 Participants: Development Team, Claude AI

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Initial conversation to establish the OSSREP project - an open-source platform for managing a Retail Electric Provider in Texas.

docs.title: Technology Stack
docs.text: Component Decision Rationale Backend Framework Quarkus Cloud-native Java, fast startup, GraalVM support Frontend Framework Angular 21 Enterprise-ready, TypeScript, strong tooling UI Components ng-bootstrap Bootstrap-based Angular components Database PostgreSQL Robust, open-source, excellent for transactional data Documentation MkDocs Material Clean, searchable, GitHub Pages compatible

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
• Microservices architecture - Separate services for distinct business domains
  • GitHub organization - ossrep to host all project repositories
  • Documentation site - Hosted at ossrep.github.io
  • Project naming - All projects prefixed with ossrep-

docs.title: Coding Standards
docs.text:
• Use Angular Signals for reactive state management
  • Use inject() function instead of constructor injection
  • Page components use .page.ts suffix (class names end in "Page")
  • Shared components use .component.ts suffix
  • Services use .service.ts suffix
  • Guards use .guard.ts suffix

docs.title: Documentation Site (ossrep.github.io)
docs.text:
• Created MkDocs Material documentation site
  • Set up GitHub Actions for automatic deployment
  • Created architecture documentation
  • Established conversation logging structure

docs.title: Customer Portal (ossrep-customer-portal)
docs.text:
Angular 21 application with the following features:

Public Pages: - Home page with plan selection - Login page - Multi-step signup wizard

Authenticated Pages: - Dashboard with account overview - Billing history and invoice viewer - Usage tracking with daily/hourly data - Payment processing - Account settings (profile, security, payment methods)

Technical Implementation: - Route guards for authentication - HTTP interceptor for JWT tokens - Services using Angular Signals - ng-bootstrap components throughout

docs.title: Repositories Created
docs.text: Repository Description Status ossrep.github.io Documentation site Ready ossrep-customer-portal Customer web application Ready

docs.title: Action Items
docs.text:
• [x] Create documentation site repository (ossrep.github.io)
  • [x] Create customer portal Angular application
  • [ ] Define microservices boundaries and create architecture diagrams
  • [ ] Create backend services (Quarkus)
  • [ ] Establish development environment setup guide
  • [ ] Define contribution guidelines

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
1. Create the backend services to support the customer portal
  2. Set up the development database
  3. Implement ERCOT/Smart Meter Texas integration services

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text: Detailed instructions for setting up your development environment for OSSREP.

docs.title: Install JDK 21
docs.text:
macOS (Homebrew)Ubuntu/DebianSDKMAN (Recommended) 
brew install openjdk@21

sudo apt install openjdk-21-jdk

sdk install java 21-tem

docs.title: Install Maven
docs.text:
Maven 3.9+ is required for building Quarkus services.

macOS (Homebrew)SDKMAN 
brew install maven

sdk install maven

docs.title: IntelliJ IDEA (Recommended)
docs.text:
1. Install the Quarkus plugin
  2. Enable annotation processing
  3. Import projects as Maven projects

docs.title: VS Code
docs.text:
1. Install "Extension Pack for Java"
  2. Install "Quarkus" extension

docs.title: Install Node.js
docs.text:
Node.js 20+ is required for Angular development.

macOS (Homebrew)nvm (Recommended) 
brew install node@20

nvm install 20
nvm use 20

docs.title: Install Angular CLI
docs.text: npm install -g @angular/cli

docs.title: Install Docker
docs.text:
Follow the official Docker installation guide for your operating system:

  • Docker Desktop for Mac
  • Docker Desktop for Windows
  • Docker Engine for Linux

docs.title: Local Infrastructure
docs.text:
Create a docker-compose.yml in your workspace root:

version: '3.8'

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ossrep
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ossrep
      POSTGRES_DB: ossrep
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data

  # Add other services as needed (Kafka, Redis, etc.)

volumes:
  postgres_data:

Start the infrastructure:

docker-compose up -d

docs.title: Connect to PostgreSQL
docs.text: psql -h localhost -U ossrep -d ossrep

docs.title: Create service databases
docs.text:
Each microservice should have its own database:

CREATE DATABASE customer_service;
CREATE DATABASE billing_service;
CREATE DATABASE usage_service;
CREATE DATABASE enrollment_service;
CREATE DATABASE product_service;

docs.title: Running Services in Dev Mode
docs.text:
Quarkus provides excellent developer experience with live reload:

cd customer-service
./mvnw quarkus:dev

Features available in dev mode:

  • Live reload - Code changes apply automatically
  • Dev UI - Available at http://localhost:8080/q/dev
  • Swagger UI - Available at http://localhost:8080/q/swagger-ui

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Common environment variables for development:

# Database
QUARKUS_DATASOURCE_JDBC_URL=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/customer_service
QUARKUS_DATASOURCE_USERNAME=ossrep
QUARKUS_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD=ossrep

# Logging
QUARKUS_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Welcome to OSSREP! This guide will help you understand the project and get started with development.

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
OSSREP is organized as a collection of repositories under the ossrep GitHub organization:

ossrep/
├── ossrep.github.io      # Documentation (this site)
├── customer-service      # Customer management
├── billing-service       # Billing and invoicing
├── usage-service         # Usage data processing
├── enrollment-service    # Enrollment and ERCOT
├── product-service       # Products and pricing
├── gateway-service       # API gateway
├── customer-portal       # Customer web application
├── admin-portal          # Operations web application
└── ossrep-commons        # Shared libraries

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, ensure you have the following installed:

Tool Version Purpose JDK 21+ Java development Maven 3.9+ Build tool Node.js 20+ Frontend development Docker 24+ Containerization Git 2.40+ Version control

docs.title: 1. Clone the repositories
docs.text:
# Create project directory
mkdir ossrep && cd ossrep

# Clone all repositories
git clone https://github.com/ossrep/ossrep.github.io.git
git clone https://github.com/ossrep/customer-service.git
# ... clone other repositories as needed

docs.title: 2. Start infrastructure
docs.text:
# Start PostgreSQL and other infrastructure
docker-compose up -d postgres

docs.title: 3. Run a service
docs.text:
cd customer-service
./mvnw quarkus:dev

The service will be available at http://localhost:8080.

docs.title: 4. Run the frontend
docs.text:
cd customer-portal
npm install
npm start

The application will be available at http://localhost:4200.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Read the Development Setup guide for detailed environment configuration
  • Review the Architecture Overview to understand the system design
  • Check individual service READMEs for service-specific documentation
